title: Bouteloua reederorum
intro: Bouteloua reederorum, also known as Reeder's grama, is a perennial grass native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Bouteloua reederorum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Bouteloua reederorum is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is used to treat conditions such as arthritis, colds, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Bouteloua reederorum is classified as a species of grass within the family Poaceae. It belongs to the tribe Andropogoneae, which is a group of grasses native to the Americas.
